stocks.recent_news: Q4 FY26 results announced April 28, 2026: Q4 revenue Rs 2,119 cr (+29.1% YoY), Q4 PBT Rs 410.85 cr (+27% YoY). FY26 revenue Rs 7,002 cr (+38% YoY), PAT Rs 747.93 cr (+41.8% YoY). Final dividend Rs 6.70/share (FY26 total Rs 19.60 incl. Rs 12.90 interim). Delivered 8 vessels to Indian Navy in FY26 incl. P-17A frigates and Survey Vessel Large ships. Signed MoU March 5, 2026 with Kalyani Strategic Systems for indigenous naval systems, USVs and AUVs.
stocks.sector_context: Indian defence shipbuilding entering a structural multi-decade upcycle on naval modernisation, indigenisation push and target 200+ ship fleet. Defence Acquisition Council approvals worth Rs 8.45 lakh cr expected to drive ~3x order inflows by FY27. Mazagon Dock soared 23% in 30 days; Cochin Shipyard, GRSE rallying alongside. Sector momentum further supported by post-conflict capex expectations.
stocks.peer_comparison: Vs Mazagon Dock (submarine focus, P-75I CNC concluded, ~Rs 99,000 cr order pipeline) and Cochin Shipyard (capital ships/MRO, Drydocks World partnership), GRSE is the volume-led modular play with growing export traction in patrol vessels/corvettes. CMP ~Rs 2,871 (May 5), 52w range Rs 1,741-3,538, market cap Rs 31,826 cr, P/E 42.55. Ashika Buy target Rs 2,730.
red_flags: Stock up ~48% in April 2026 ahead of results - near-term technical extension
Elevated P/E ~42x leaves limited margin for execution slip
catalysts: 110th AGM and final dividend Rs 6.70 payment within 30 days of declaration
Upcoming MoD order awards on Rs 8.45 lakh cr DAC pipeline
Sell-side target revisions post strong Q4 print

stocks.recent_news: Q4 FY26 board meeting May 4, 2026; earnings call held May 5. Highest-ever quarterly revenue Rs 395.22 cr (+11% YoY), PAT Rs 125-126 cr (+10.88% YoY). Final dividend Rs 4/share recommended; record date July 10, 2026; AGM July 7, 2026. Cash and equivalents Rs 854.45 cr. Stock surged ~20% in the last month; brokerages reiterated 'Buy' post-results.
stocks.sector_context: Mutual fund RTA business benefiting from continued growth in domestic MF AUM and SIP inflows. CAMS services ~68-69% of industry assets. Non-MF businesses (insurance repository, payments, KRA, account aggregator) scaling and diversifying revenue mix. Duopoly market structure with KFin Technologies remains intact.
stocks.peer_comparison: CAMS is the dominant duopoly RTA with materially larger MF asset share than KFin (which has more AMC clients but smaller asset pool). CMP Rs 826.8 on May 7, 2026 (+10.5% over 6 months, +11.98% YoY). P/E ~46, market cap ~Rs 20,200 cr. Trading mid-range of 52w Rs 611-875.
red_flags: Concentration risk if SEBI tightens TER/RTA-fee regulation
Limited diversification beyond core MF RTA business
catalysts: AGM July 7, 2026 and dividend record date July 10, 2026
FY27 management guidance from earnings call
Continued SIP and MF AUM growth flowing through to fee revenue

stocks.recent_news: Board meeting scheduled May 8, 2026 to approve FY26 audited financial results. Analyst and investor earnings call May 8 at 3:30 PM IST hosted by DAM Capital Advisors. Operating subsidiary CIFCL reported Q4 disbursements Rs 32,913 cr (+25% YoY) and AUM Rs 2,42,630 cr. Trading window restrictions per SEBI were in effect April 1-10. Quarterly SEBI compliance certificate filed April 10 confirmed regulatory compliance for Q4 FY26.
stocks.sector_context: Diversified NBFC/financial-services holding company with vehicle finance (CIFCL), general insurance (Chola MS) and risk services. NBFC sector under monitoring for asset quality after a strong AUM growth cycle. Vehicle finance benefits from steady CV and used-car demand; general insurance arm rides P&C growth.
stocks.peer_comparison: CHOLAHLDNG is the parent holdco of CIFCL, Chola MS General Insurance and Chola MS Risk Services. Trades at a holding-company discount versus Bajaj Finserv. CIFCL's Q4 disbursement growth (+25%) is in line with sector leaders. Stock-level performance lags pure-play CIFCL.
red_flags: Binary event risk - earnings tomorrow May 8 could trigger sharp move either way
Holding-company discount can widen if subsidiary commentary disappoints
Sector watch for asset-quality slippage in vehicle/MSME book
catalysts: FY26 audited results and analyst call on May 8, 2026

stocks.recent_news: Board meeting today May 7, 2026 to approve Q4 and FY26 audited results. Q4 FY26 net profit Rs 702.25 cr (+37.56% YoY), revenue Rs 8,044 cr (+13.7%). FY26 revenue Rs 31,149 cr (vs Rs 28,153 cr), PAT Rs 2,426 cr. Final dividend of Rs 229/share recommended. Company Secretary S. Dhanvanth Kumar resigned effective today (health reasons); Thulsidass T. V. appointed VP, General Counsel and Company Secretary. Q3 FY26 PAT was Rs 691.83 cr (+119% YoY).
stocks.sector_context: Indian tyre sector benefiting from softer raw material costs (rubber, crude derivatives) and steady OEM/replacement demand. Margin expansion broad-based across the sector in FY26. Premium tyre pricing remains competitive among Apollo, CEAT, Balkrishna and JK Tyre. Operating margin lifted to ~12% in Q3 FY26 from 6% YoY.
stocks.peer_comparison: MRF remains the largest Indian tyre maker by revenue. Despite robust Q3 (+119% PAT) and Q4 (+37.6% PAT) prints, share price has lagged: CMP ~Rs 1,28,950 - down 18.3% over 6 months and -5.06% over 1 year. Peer Apollo/CEAT/Balkrishna have generally fared better in relative-return terms.
red_flags: Senior compliance officer (CS) resignation today on results day - though stated reason is health
Stock has materially underperformed peers despite strong earnings - persistent valuation/competitive concerns
Highest absolute share price stock in India - low retail liquidity amplifies volatility
catalysts: Q4 and FY26 results today with Rs 229 final dividend recommendation
Continued raw material tailwind into FY27
AGM and dividend payout in coming months

stocks.recent_news: Stock surged 7.45% on May 5, 2026 outperforming Auto Components sector by 7.21pp; gained another 5.51% on May 6 indicating momentum reversal from sideways to bullish. Board meeting May 13, 2026 to approve Q4 and FY26 results and consider final dividend; earnings call confirmed May 14, 2026 11:00 AM IST (Group CEO Mahendra Goyal, MD Atul Jaggi, CFO Mohit Srivastava). H1 FY26 revenue Rs 2,278.7 cr (+14.7% YoY), EBITDA Rs 224.6 cr (9.9% margin), PAT Rs 131 cr. Promoter shareholding update (Feb 2026) reflected intra-family transmission from late Mr. Deep C Anand to Mrs. Kiran D Anand - not pledge.
stocks.sector_context: Indian auto components sector facing headwind from US 26% reciprocal tariff on Indian manufactured goods (April 2, 2026), creating uncertainty for export-exposed names. Domestic OEM demand mixed; ride-control/suspension segment dependent on PV and 2W cycles. Some peers seeing China-plus-one tailwinds offsetting tariff drag. Bharat Forge (~Rs 65,000 cr m-cap) up ~19.5% in last month on defence/aerospace tailwinds.
stocks.peer_comparison: Gabriel India is the leading shock absorber, strut and front-fork supplier. Market cap ~Rs 15,876 cr. CMP ~Rs 1,105 (May 5); 1-year return +102.31% but 6-month return -12.67% reflecting sector consolidation. Smaller scale than Endurance Tech (~Rs 16,000 cr, P/E 32-50x, ROE 14.6%) and Bharat Forge but pure-play on ride control. Recent two-day outperformance suggests relative strength.
red_flags: US 26% reciprocal tariff on Indian goods (April 2, 2026) overhangs export-linked revenue
Earnings binary event in 6 days - elevated event risk
Recent +13% two-day rally raises near-term mean-reversion risk into results
catalysts: Q4 FY26 results board meeting May 13, 2026 with final dividend consideration
Earnings call May 14, 2026 - FY27 commentary and tariff response expected
Recent bullish technical reversal on rising volume
